questions.question: Which company has delivered the first lot of light bulletproof vehicles to the Indian Air Force, in collaboration with Lockheed Martin?
questions.explanation: Ashok Leyland, which is a flagship firm of the Hinduja group, has delivered the first lot of light bulletproof vehicles to the Indian Air Force. These vehicles have been designed and developed in collaboration with Lockheed Martin, which is a US based aerospace and defence technology company.

questions.question: What is the name of the effect which describes cities being warmer than surrounding rural areas?
questions.explanation: The “Urban heat island effect” describes cities being warmer than surrounding rural areas. It means that darker, paved surfaces absorb more heat than more natural surroundings. A recent study found that 94% of lower income communities of color, are exposed to higher temperatures than affluent areas. This is because these lower income communities lack green space and suffer most from urban heat islands.

questions.question: Tehri lake, on whose bank an Adventure Sports Institute has been opened, is in which state?
questions.explanation: Uttarakhand Chief Minister Tirath Singh Rawat and Union Minister Kiren Rijiju inaugurated a water sports and adventure institute on the bank of the Tehri lake. The institute will be run by the Indo Tibetan Border Police and has been built at a cost of Rs 20 crore. The institute is built at a cost of Rs 20 crore and will be run by the Indo Tibetan Border Police.

questions.question: Sivasubramanian Ramann, who was making news recently, took over as the Chairman and MD of which organisation?
questions.explanation: Small Industries Development Bank of India (SIDBI) announced that Sivasubramanian Ramann has taken charge as Chairman and Managing Director of the bank. The appointment is for a period of three years. Prior to this appointment, Ramann was serving as MD and CEO of National E-Governance Services Ltd (NeSL). He has also held the position of Executive Director in Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI).

questions.question: “Blue Nature Alliance”, which was making news recently, is associated with which field?
questions.explanation: A new global marine initiative has been launched to protect and conserve 18 million square kilometres of the ocean over the next five years. This initiative is known as the “Blue Nature Alliance”. It is led by several philanthropic organisations along with national governments, local communities, Indigenous peoples, scientists, and academics.

questions.question: AIIPOIP Grant Programme has been launched by Australia in partnership with which country?
questions.explanation: The High Commissioner of Australia to India has launched the Australia-India Indo-Pacific Oceans Initiative Partnership (AIIPOIP) grant program. This AUD 1.4 million (INR 8.12 crore) grant program aims to support a free, open Indo-Pacific region The programme would encourage exchange of expertise and resources between Australian and Indian stakeholders that complements regional mechanisms such as ASEAN, the Indian Ocean Rim Association, Pacific Islands Forum etc.

questions.question: ‘Nimbus’ is a flagship cloud service project of which country?
questions.explanation: ‘Nimbus’ is a flagship cloud service project of Israel, which aims to provide a solution for cloud services to the government, the defence system, and other sectors. Recently, Israel’s government chose Amazon Web Services (AWS) and Google for the USD 1-billion project to provide cloud services for the country’s public sector and military. Amazon and Google beat Microsoft, Oracle, and IBM in the tender for the four-phase “Nimbus” project.

questions.question: ‘COVIRAP’ technology has been developed by which institute?
questions.explanation: The Indian Institute of Technology – Kharagpur has commercialized its healthcare product COVIRAP. This is a diagnostic technology to zero on Covid infections. Using this technology, the COVID test results can be obtained within 45 minutes of sampling and the results can be seen using a smartphone application.

questions.question: The Union health ministry advised which position to be used by the Covid patients for better breathing?
questions.explanation: The Union health ministry of India advised ‘proning for self-care’ for coronavirus patients, as it is considered extremely beneficial for the patients in home isolation with breathing difficulty. Proning is the process of turning a patient with safe motions, from their back onto their abdomen so that the individual is lying face down. This position improves breathing comfort and oxygenation. It is useful when the patient feels difficulty in breathing and the SpO2 decreases below 94.

questions.question: When is the “International Day of Multilateralism and Diplomacy for Peace” observed across the world?
questions.explanation: The “International Day of Multilateralism and Diplomacy for Peace” is observed on April 24, every year across the world. On 12 December 2018, the General Assembly adopted the resolution, “International Day of Multilateralism and Diplomacy for Peace. It was first observed on April 24, 2019.
